text: Literally, ‘that you may know that I, the Lord, am your God’.

This seems to be a proverb, which reads in the Hebrew text, ‘to carry off the well-watered with the thirsty’. The idolater foresees that the whole people will suffer for his guilt, having probably in the mean time imitated his bad example.
Verses 22-29 as well as chapter 30, seem to follow directly upon the thought of chapter 28, and verses 1-21 should perhaps be regarded as a kind of parenthesis.
